mnemonics: Wait Patiently: WaitPID allows a parent to wait for a specific child process to change state, identified by its PID.
keyPoints: WaitPID allows a parent process to wait for a specific child process using its Process ID (PID).
It provides more control than wait(), including options like WNOHANG to return immediately if no child has exited.
It returns the PID of the terminated child and stores the exit status in the provided integer pointer.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to check the return value of waitpid(), leading to unhandled zombie processes.
Incorrectly using the options flag (e.g., using 0 instead of WNOHANG) or misusing the PID argument (e.g., using -1 to wait for any child).
